---
title: "Equations"
author: "Deon Roos"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

Question:

For a linear model with a categorical variable which includes the category group, with levels “A1”, “A2”, “A3” and “A4”, which of the following corresponds to the equivalent equation for the code lm(y ~ group, data = my_data)?

Answer 1 (Correct):

$y_i ∼ Normal(\mu_i, \sigma)$

$\mu_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \times A2_i + \beta_2 \times A3_i + \beta_3 \times A4_i$

Answer 2:

$y_i ∼ Normal(\mu_i, \sigma)$

$\mu_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \times A1_i + \beta_2 \times A2_i + \beta_3 \times A3_i$

Answer 3:

$y_i ∼ Normal(\mu_i, \sigma)$

$\mu_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \times A1_i + \beta_2 \times A2_i + \beta_3 \times A3_i + \beta_4 \times A4_i$

Answer 4:

$y_i ∼ Normal(\mu_i, \sigma)$

$\mu_i = \beta_0 \times A1_i + \beta_1 \times A2_i + \beta_2 \times A3_i + \beta_3 \times A4_i$
